nodes.content:
There are three rules for creating links:

1) **Linked thoughts must exist** 
       (at least at the time of creation)
2) **Links can only ever point backwards in time**
      (how can you link to something that doesn't exist?)
3) **A thought can have anything from 0 to 3 links**
      (but it can get more replies than you can count!)

[links_and_replies][Go back to "Links and Replies"]

Click on either of the links below to learn more.

- [the_project][More information about the project]

- [cookies_and_privacy_policy][Our privacy policy]

- [contact][Contact]


  [hello_explorer][Go back to the beginning]

To continue, you must be at least 13 years of age.

   [i_am_13_years_or_older][I am 13 years or older]
   
   [i_am_not_yet_old_enough][I am not yet old enough]
   

[almost_there!][Back to "Almost there!"]

The second rule is very simple: **Be respectful**
 
When we disagree, that is grounds for a discussion, not animosity.

If you are here, there is something human about you - put some of that in here - your projects, your day, share what you find interesting, criticise what you deem worthy of a critique.

Just don't waste time and space on aimless arguing, troll feeding, culture wars crap and, please, don't use AI to generate thoughts.

Aphantasia has three rules:

[be_yourself][1) Be yourself]

[be_respectful][2) Be respectful]

[use_your_head][3) Use your head]


[i_am_13_years_or_older][Back to "Yes"]

We store the following:
- Account data (registered users only)
	- **Username**
	- **Password hash**
	- **Email** (if provided)
	- **User settings**
	- Any created **content**
- Technical logs related to hosting - notably:
	- **IP address**
	- **Date and time of requests**
	- **Operating system**
	- **Browser/client type**

Access **logs are stored for up to one month** since creation, after which they are **deleted**.

For any Aphantasia-related needs send us a mail to:

aphantastic[dot]channel[at]gmail[dot]com

**Cookies**

This website uses **strictly functional cookies** for:
- Persistent login
- Persistent settings (such as graph size)

We use **no third party cookies or services**.
 
  **Your data**

**Collected data is not** and will not be **sold to third parties** nor will it be used for tasks unrelated to development, maintenance or promotion of the website.

Note however, that any **content that you create is public** and therefore free for anyone to use as they see fit.

[collected_data][Click here to see which data we collect]

  **Account deletion**
  
Users can delete their account immediately and permanently from the main menu.

However, any content reproduced by others (mentions, quotes, replies...) will remain intact.
Then you may continue.

nodes.text: I am not yet old enough
nodes.content:
Then you may not enter.

Please, close this page and go about your usual business.

This is an [attributions][attribution].

Obsidian is a note taking app that first introduced me to a graph view. It was the initial inspiration for Aphantasia and I used it to write the text you are reading right now.

https://obsidian.md/

Aphantasia is a project aiming to create a self-hosted social space focused on humans and what they have to say.

**The code is open source** and can be found here:
https://github.com/0rbit3r/aphantasia
  (server and web app)
https://github.com/0rbit3r/grafika
  (a Javascript library powering Aphantasia's graph view)
  
  
Accompanying youtube channel:
https://www.youtube.com/@AphantasticChannel

This place is 13+.

As such, please try to act in ways, that promote a safe environment.

If you rather have it spelled out:
__porn, gore, violence, spam, propaganda, racism, sexism__... did I miss any?
          ...**don't belong on Aphantasia**.

Just **use your head** and you will be fine. You're a smart cookie. 

**I believe in you;-)**

**The building block of Aphantasia is a thought**.

A thought, just like a comment or a forum post, is just **a piece of text** written by a user.

This thought, for example, has a title **About Thoughts** and was written by user named **Green**.

 [join][Go back to "Join"]

Now you are ready to join Aphantasia.

But before you do we need to go through a few formalities.

[link_me!][Back to "Link me!"]

This project is named after a fascinating phenomena.

People with aphantasia (sometimes called aphants) are unable to visualize mental images.

How does one imagine when their imagination cannot see?



They create a website where they can visualise thoughts with others, of course:-P

[trivia][Trivia]

So you are a true explorer, huh?  [register][Registration can wait]

Here you can find more information about the project, its features, limitations promises and whatever else I might wanna add here.

Now that you are both done and validated for creating your first thought, let's create your first association.

**To proceed, connect me to Red.**

He should have a thought floating around here somewhere.

**Thoughts grow as they gather replies.**

This is good to know. **Whatever you link to**, whether you praise, criticise, argue or support, a link **will always bump the size** of its target.

[mechanics_and_features][Mechanics and Features]

**Welcome to Aphantasia!**

You are now on a path to create an account and join the hivemind.

Simply **click on the hollow circles and follow the instructions** and eventually you will find the registration form.

[hello_explorer][Go back to the beginning]

But enough info-dumping, **let's create a practice thought**.

Take a look at the **big square** at the top of the screen - that's your **Mode** selection **button**. It's currently showing you that you are in the "Welcome mode".

**Click the Mode button, and select "Write"** to open the thought creation tool.

[non-linearity][or go back to "Non-linearity" instead]

[about_thoughts][Go back to "About Thoughts"]

The **underlined text** two lines above **is** called **a link**.

**Links** reference the past and **can** be used to:
- **continue** a chain of thought (like I'm doing now)
- **mention** what you've talked about
- **respond** to what others have said
- **associate** multiple thoughts together


**Reply is the opposite end of link** and in practice it's a way to explore forwards in time. 

**This thought has two** replies you can click on:

A consequence of the [3_axioms_of_links][3 axioms of links] and the key to mastering [reading_the_graph][Reading the graph] is __non linearity__.

Take a look at this thought in the graph view - since **it links two thoughts at once, it can be arrived to from two different directions**.

When you get to exploring Aphantasia, you will see this pattern everywhere - **thoughts reply, continue or expand** on other thoughts, **sometimes all at once**.

This is an [attributions][attribution].

PixiJS is a graphics library for Javascript upon which I built Grafika - the graph engine powering Aphantasia.

https://pixijs.com/

The colorful animation you hopefully see is called a **graph view**.

Now, notice that in the graph view the links are asymmetric - the replied thought is always at the wide base of the connection while **the linking thought gets the narrow end**.

In other words, if you imagine the link to be an **arrow**, it **will always point towards the future**.

[links_and_replies][Go back to "Links and Replies"]

This thought is an [attributions][attribution].

SQL Climber is a website that aims to teach you SQL from scratch by giving you homework.

Years ago I told its author that I was gonna make a website too one day. And that once I do, I will put link to SQL Climber on there.

So if you are interested in learning SQL for free, check out https://sqlclimber.com

This is an [attributions][attribution].

SolidJS is a Javascript framework reminiscent of React, but sleeker and, in my humble opinion, easier to work with and reason about.

Solid was used to create the UI of Aphantasia (everything but the graph view).

https://www.solidjs.com/

[hello_explorer][Go back]

`Login form will be here`
[use_your_head][Back to "Use your head]

Before you [register][Register], let's have a little PSA.

**Do not reuse passwords**

That is a great general advice, not just one for websites hosted by some rando.

Your **browser** likely already supports **password** management and **generation** - take advantage of it.

[be_respectful][My foot, you pretentious dolt]

While the rules are in place for a reason...

sometimes it's fun being someone else.

Some things are so bad and stupid it needs to be said.

And sometimes, you just gotta turn that brain off...

This is not an invitation to break the code of conduct... only not to follow it too rigidly.

All **in good faith** of course;-)

Hello I'm Red.

Would you be so kind and connect me to my friend Green over there?

**Simply go to the thought creator, click the Link button and then click on this thought in the graph view.**

**Then do the same for thought titled "Good job!"**

**Then publish to proceed**

Or lack there of...

**Users can create only 3 thoughts a day.**

Harsh rule I know... but think of all the money I will save on database space:3

[mechanics_and_features][Mechanics and Features]

This thought is an [attributions][attribution].

Your world of text is a simple but endearing idea - an infinite canvas of text editable by anyone.

I used to promote Aphantasia on there so it's only fair to return the favor.

Check out https://yourworldoftext.com

If I were to name everyone and every thing that made Aphantasia possible, I would still be listing today.

But I would like to give a little shout out to at least a few, so here it goes.


[explore_some_more?][Explore some more?] is a thought.
[explore_some_more?][Explore some more?] is where you came from.
[explore_some_more?][Explore some more?] is a link
